title: ## **Balance Sheet Overview – As of March 31, 2026**
content:
# Balance Sheet Composition
The most significant balance-sheet development this period was the contraction in investment securities holdings.
Total assets stood at $217.9 million as of March 31, 2026, declining -1.0%.
The largest asset category moved down to $78.0 million for Investment Securities, a decrease of -15.2%, resulting in a smaller investment holding.
Cash and Deposits grew to $107.9 million, up 15.7%. Loans and Leases declined to $27.7 million, falling -8.7%.
The remaining categories moved more modestly over the period.

# Funding Sources
Investment Securities is now the largest component of the balance sheet. That growth on the asset side was funded primarily through Shares/Deposits, reflecting how the institution's holdings were paid for during the period.
Total liabilities stood at $149.0 million, increasing 2.9%, led by Shares/Deposits at $147.4 million, up 3.1%. The remaining categories moved more modestly over the period.
The additional assets were funded mainly through member deposits.

# Capital and Synthesis
Total equity stood at $29.6 million, increasing 2.2%, supported by undivided earnings of $0.4 million and growing reserves. Taken together, the institution's retained capital base grew while its investment portfolio shrank significantly relative to cash holdings. Management continues to watch the pace of loan growth relative to deposit growth.
